_doc: Topic registry — the routing brain of /v1/ask. Each topic carries (a) `description` — a short paragraph used to compute a sentence-transformer embedding for semantic routing of free-text questions, (b) `bands[]` — canonical bands the topic concerns, served by /v1/recall, (c) `algorithms[]` — composition recipes from algorithms-v0.json applied on top of those bands, (d) `aliases[]` — short example phrases that frequently appear in real questions for this topic; useful both as additional embedding-side training data and as a substring fallback when the transformer is offline. Routing is by cosine similarity (transformer mode) or substring search over `aliases[]` + `key` (keyword fallback mode). New topics land here, not in code. Replaces the static TOPIC_KEYWORDS / TOPIC_BANDS / TOPIC_ALGORITHMS tables that lived in crates/emem-api-rest/src/lib.rs through 0.0.2; shipped as a content-addressed registry in 0.0.3.

_routing.fallback_when_offline: substring search over aliases[] and key

_backends.ort: default (since 2026-05-04). Direct `ort` 2.x + `tokenizers` BERT inference using BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 (110 M params, 768-D, MTEB ~63). Reads tokenizer.json + model.onnx from EMEM_TOPIC_MODEL_DIR (default <EMEM_DATA>/models/bge-base-en-v1.5/). CLS-pooled, L2-normalised. ~110 ms warm /v1/ask end-to-end on CPU. GPU off by default — the bundled CPU runtime is fast enough for a 25-topic registry; CUDA dylib re-enable via EMEM_TOPIC_USE_GPU=1 + a working libonnxruntime.so.
_backends.model2vec: fallback. Static-distillation token-lookup embedder (minishlab/potion-base-8M, 256-D, ~32 MB, sub-µs per query, no ONNX dep). Selected automatically when ort cannot load. Also the EMEM_TOPIC_BACKEND=transformer alias.
_backends.keyword: deterministic substring search over aliases[] + key. No model. Selected by EMEM_TOPIC_BACKEND=keyword for unit tests / air-gapped builds. Also runs as a precision pre-pass in front of ort and model2vec so exact-match nouns always surface even when the cosine score is below threshold.

env_overrides.EMEM_TOPIC_BACKEND: ort (default) | model2vec | keyword. Legacy aliases `fastembed`→`ort` and `transformer`→`model2vec` still resolve.
env_overrides.EMEM_TOPIC_MODEL_DIR: where the ort backend reads tokenizer.json and model.onnx from (default <EMEM_DATA>/models/bge-base-en-v1.5/)
env_overrides.EMEM_TOPIC_USE_GPU: set to 1 to attempt CUDAExecutionProvider for the ort backend; needs a working libonnxruntime.so (today the bundled CPU runtime is what we use)

topics.description: Whether a place has flooded in the past — long-term flood history from satellite-observed surface water occurrence over decades. Answers questions about a location's historical flood record, not present or future risk.

Whether a place is wet, flooded, or under standing water right now or in the last few days, observed via radar (Sentinel-1) and optical water indices (NDWI, MNDWI). Answers acute event-window questions about lakes, ponds, reservoirs, tanks, wetlands, marshes, lagoons, and current flood inundation.
aliases: is this man-made lake at risk

topics.description: Forward-looking flood-risk question for a place — will this area flood, is it flood-prone, should I buy a flat or build here given flood exposure, monsoon waterlogging risk, real-estate flood due-diligence. Composes long-term water occurrence, elevation, and recent radar water into a probability of future flooding. Distinct from flood history (past) and event window (now).
aliases: is this area at risk of flooding
buying a flat is it flood-prone
buying a home in this area
should I buy property here flood
is it safe to invest in property
is this place going to flood

topics.description: Plant health, greenness, canopy water content, and stress at a place — leaf-area, biomass, photosynthetic activity. Answers questions about vegetation, crops, forest condition, NDVI/EVI, and plant stress.

Whether a place burned recently, how severely, and post-fire recovery — and whether a candidate location is wildfire-safe. Uses the Normalized Burn Ratio (NBR) and dNBR for burn-scar detection and severity classification. Answers questions about wildfire risk, burn scars, fire damage, post-fire vegetation, and climate-haven-from-fire siting.
Whether the surface is exposed bare soil vs. vegetated or built-up. Uses the Bare Soil Index. Answers questions about exposed earth, fallow land, post-harvest fields, and bare-soil cover.

Whether a place is urbanized — building footprints, road network, point-of-interest density, and built-up index. Answers questions about urbanization, settlement, infrastructure, building density, and transportation networks.

Present-time weather at a place — temperature, cloud cover, precipitation, wind. Live nowcast from numerical weather prediction. Answers questions about current weather, what's the temperature, is it raining, wind speed, cloud cover.
Elevation including ocean depth (bathymetry) and land topography from a global merged dataset. Answers questions about depth, bathymetry, ocean floor, and land elevation in a single layer.
aliases: how deep is the ocean here

topics.description: Land-surface elevation above sea level from Copernicus DEM (Cop-DEM GLO-30). Answers questions about altitude, height above sea level, and terrain elevation on land.

Terrain shape derived from elevation — slope, ruggedness, topographic position, distance to coast. Answers questions about terrain steepness, mountainousness, ridge-vs-valley position, coastal proximity.

Air quality, heat exposure, and environmental health risks at a place. Air quality (PM2.5, NO2, O3, CO, AOD), land surface temperature, and humidity. Answers questions about whether the air is safe, if it's dangerously hot, heat-vulnerability, and outdoor exposure for vulnerable groups (kids, elderly, pregnant women).

Crop and pasture monitoring at a place — vegetation indices for crop type, growth stage, stress, yield proxy, sowing/harvest detection, growing degree days, nitrogen uptake. Answers questions about farming, agriculture, crops, yield, planting, harvest, and pasture management.

Soil properties at a place from SoilGrids 2.0 — soil organic carbon, pH, clay, sand, bulk density, nitrogen at the topsoil layer, plus soil moisture, salinity, and erosion proxies. Answers questions about soil quality, fertility, salinity, erosion risk, and land degradation.

Carbon-stock change for credit issuance at a place — VM0042 carbon credits, EUDR compliance, soil organic carbon change, rice-methane reduction, forest cover loss. Answers questions about carbon farming, MRV, voluntary carbon market, EUDR deforestation due-diligence, and rice methane offsets.

Parametric weather/climate triggers and crop-loss assessment at a place — drought (SPI), excess rainfall, heat events, cyclone exposure for parametric insurance products. Answers questions about insurance triggers, parametric payouts, drought events, crop loss after a weather event.
Snow cover at a place from the Normalized Difference Snow Index (NDSI). Answers questions about snowpack, snow cover, ski conditions, and tick-season delay due to lingering snow.

Environmental, social, and governance signals at a place — carbon sink, biodiversity proxy, deforestation, fire frequency, air pollution exposure, land cover change. Answers ESG due-diligence questions about a property, supply-chain location, or operational site.

Property climate-risk and physical-hazard score for a place — flood, heat, coastal erosion, urban canopy, walkability for real-estate buying, valuation, or insurance. Answers questions about whether a property is climate-safe, physical risk score, insurance premium proxy.
aliases: is this neighborhood safe to live in

topics.description: How livable an urban neighborhood is at a place — walkability, bike-friendliness, green-space access, urban tree canopy, outdoor comfort. Answers questions about neighborhood quality, daily-life quality, walkability, parks nearby, livability ranking.
aliases: is it pleasant to live here

topics.description: Foundation-model embedding of a place — Tessera or AlphaEarth-class universal place vector for similarity, novelty, and archetype matching. Answers questions about places similar to this place, find lookalikes, novel/anomalous regions.
aliases: what does this place look like geographically

topics.description: Raw Sentinel-2 reflectance bands and rendered RGB scene at a place. Returns blue, green, red, near-infrared, SWIR-1, SWIR-2 surface reflectance values for custom band math (NDVI, custom indices) AND a viewable Sentinel-2 RGB image. Answers questions about satellite imagery, satellite pictures, optical scenes, and what the place looks like from space.
aliases: what does this place look like

topics.description: Sentinel-2 Scene Classification Layer at a place — categorical class per pixel: cloud, cloud shadow, vegetation, bare soil, water, snow. Answers questions about pixel-level scene type and cloud masking.
Sentinel-1 C-band SAR backscatter at a place — works at night and through clouds. Answers questions about radar imagery, SAR pictures, all-weather monitoring, deformation, soil moisture proxy.

Time-series analytics over a place — volatility, trend strength, anomaly detection, change-point detection on the foundation embedding or NDVI history. Answers questions about whether this place is changing, is it a hotspot, is there a recent regime shift.

Coastal and inland water quality from Sentinel-2 at 10 m: chlorophyll-a, turbidity, satellite-derived bathymetry, and aquaculture-pond detection. Answers questions about how clean or shallow a body of water is, whether algal blooms or sediment plumes are visible, and where fish or shrimp farms operate.
